answers.retention: 10 days — the recommended rethink window before a captured conversation log is pruned.

accept.reason: the canon home's curation local pack deliberately shares the checks/ engine lib (findings, lines, context) — the same named cross-cutting concern as the packs/ acceptance; in the home checkout the engine is the repo's own tracked tree, not a consumer mount, so the reach is inherent

answers.goals: Keep extending.md's engine/content boundary structural: core (checks/, migrations/, mount/, routines/, .github/, the top-level docs, and the shared engine modules under packs/ and skills/) must never name a specific pack or skill — packs and skills are discovered content, and a core file citing one couples the engine to its content. One rule per namespace (packs/*, skills/*); reviewed inherent couplings live in each rule's except.

rules.reason: core is the engine that runs pack content — it must not name the specific packs it runs (extending.md)

except.reason: decouple(release-infra): the core .github/ README documents the chrome-extension-release plumbing being vendored out of core (#276)
decouple(release-infra): chrome-extension-release reusable release infra hosted in the core .github/ tree; being vendored out (#276)
decouple(release-infra): chrome-extension-release reusable release infra hosted in the core .github/ tree; being vendored out (#276)
decouple(release-infra): chrome-extension-release reusable release infra hosted in the core .github/ tree; being vendored out (#276)
decouple(release-infra): a chrome-release composite action in the core .github/ tree; being vendored out (#276)
inherent: a one-time seed migration necessarily names the single pack it seeds; it self-retires once the fleet converges
inherent: a one-time vendoring migration necessarily names the pack whose plumbing it relocates into consumers; it self-retires once the fleet converges

inherent(#385): the adoption-interview machinery is bundled in the adoption skill (packs/grow_with_claudinite/skills/adopt-claudinite/) by owner decision; the runner resolves it fail-soft and stays inert when the pack is absent
inherent(#385): the engine map documents where the adoption-interview machinery lives now that it is bundled in the adoption skill

accept.reason: pack check modules deliberately share the checks/ engine lib — a named cross-cutting concern per skills/file-placement/SKILL.md; packs are organized by pack, not by dependency

a skill's check-the-work modules deliberately share the checks/ engine lib (findings.mjs) — the same named cross-cutting concern as the packs/ acceptance; checks are co-located with the skill whose action they validate, not organized by dependency

the core planner deliberately imports the pack-discovery registry (engine/pack_loader/pack-registry.mjs — core machinery per extending.md, not a specific pack) to enumerate each repo's run_daily tasks; discovery lives under packs/ and the planner under routines/, so the cross-tree reach is inherent and correct, not a misplacement

the member-declaration read deliberately imports the one shared id-extractor (packEntryId in engine/pack_loader/pack-registry.mjs — core machinery per extending.md, not a specific pack) so the fleet's activePacks normalization and the engine can never drift on the declaration forms; the cross-tree reach is inherent, the same as the planner's registry import

the engine's file-set builder deliberately imports the consumer-layout constant SHARED_SUBDIR (beside LOCAL_PACKS_SUBDIR in engine/pack_loader/pack-registry.mjs — core machinery per extending.md, not a specific pack) so the shared-mount sweep exclusion and the vendor writer share one definition; the cross-tree reach is inherent, not a misplacement

the canon-side migration registry deliberately builds on the vendored sync surface in the engine lib (engine/checks/helpers/active-migrations.mjs — the home pack-independence mandates, since pack checks consult migrationActive and packs import only the engine surface); one definition, re-exported for canon callers, so the cross-tree reach is inherent, not a misplacement

the vendor-set computation deliberately imports the import-surface facts from the engine lib (engine/checks/helpers/module-imports.mjs — the one home the pack-independence check judges against), so "what a pack may import" and "what a vendor set must carry" share one definition; the cross-tree reach is inherent, not a misplacement
